channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Lockheed Martin Deflects F-35 Query on India, Signals No Direct Engagement Without Government-to-Government Process
item.description: Lockheed Martin has maintained a guarded and procedural stance regarding any potential engagement with India on the F-35 Lightning II, underscoring that all discussions related to the fifth-generation fighter must occur strictly through government-to-government channels under the U.S. Foreign Military Sales (FMS) framework.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy’s Deck-Based CCA Concept Signals Leap Toward Carrier-Launched Unmanned Air Power
item.description: A newly surfaced concept image is sparking significant interest in India’s naval aviation community, suggesting that the Indian Navy may be evaluating not one but two distinct carrier-capable Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) designs. If the concept reflects actual planning, it points toward a major doctrinal shift where unmanned combat aircraft become integral to carrier air wings.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Ashok Leyland Defence Systems Enters Unmanned and Autonomous Warfare Domain with UGVs, UAVs and Counter-Drone Solutions
item.description: Ashok Leyland Defence Systems has officially expanded its portfolio beyond conventional military mobility platforms, confirming its entry into the rapidly evolving domain of unmanned and autonomous systems. The move signals a strategic shift toward future battlefield technologies, where robotics, artificial intelligence, and network-centric operations are becoming central to combat effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF Clears Tejas Fleet for Operations After Software Fix, Jets Return to Skies Following February Mishap
item.description: The Indian Air Force (IAF) has cleared its fleet of Tejas Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) to resume full flight operations after a temporary grounding triggered by a February 7 incident linked to a software malfunction. The decision follows an extensive technical and safety review conducted jointly by the IAF and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), restoring confidence in one of India’s most important indigenous fighter programs.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: InsideFPV Delivers Kamikaze FPV Drones to Indian Army Under Emergency Procurement, Boosting High-Altitude Strike Capability
item.description: In a significant development reflecting the Indian Army’s accelerating adoption of loitering munitions and tactical unmanned systems, Surat-based drone manufacturer InsideFPV has reportedly delivered a consignment of kamikaze drones valued at approximately ?10 crore. The delivery, completed within just two months of contract signing in December, was executed under the Ministry of Defence’s emergency procurement route, underscoring the urgency of equipping frontline units with rapidly deployable precision-strike capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Godrej Aerospace Showcases Reverse-Bleed System Expertise at AESQ Forum 2026, Strengthens Position in Global Aero Engine Supply Chain
item.description: Godrej Aerospace has reinforced its growing role in the global aero engine manufacturing ecosystem by participating in the Aerospace Engine Supplier Quality (AESQ) Forum 2026 in Singapore, a high-level platform hosted by leading engine OEMs including Pratt & Whitney, GE Aerospace, Rolls-Royce, and Safran. The event serves as a focal point for advancing quality, safety, and standardization across the highly demanding aerospace propulsion supply chain.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dhash Defense Systems to Unveil Indigenous All-Terrain Mobility Platforms to Strengthen India’s Tactical Ground Capabilities
item.description: Dhash Defense Systems has officially announced its entry into India’s defence mobility segment with the launch of its indigenously designed, developed, and manufactured (IDDM) all-terrain ground mobility platforms. Positioned as rugged, high-performance vehicles tailored for military applications, these systems aim to enhance tactical mobility across diverse and demanding operational environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy handed over 100 INSAS rifles & 50,000 rounds to Kenyan Navy
item.description: India handed over 100 INSAS rifles, 50,000 rounds of ammunition, and a 1.5 Tesla MRI machine to the Kenya Defence Forces during the port call of Indian Naval Ship (INS) Trikand at Mombasa Port, officials said on Wednesday. The visit, running from April 7 to 10, is part of INS Trikand’s operational deployment in the Western Indian Ocean Region and is accompanied by Vice Admiral K. Swaminathan, Flag Officer Commanding-in-Chief of India’s Western Naval Command.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Tata Lockheed Martin exploring potential investment opportunities in Telangana.
item.description: he Managing Director of Tata Lockheed Martin Aerostructure Ltd (TLMAL), Tim Riffle, paid a courtesy visit to Chief Minister Revanth Reddy. Lockheed Martin is planning to set up a C-130J Super Hercules final assembly line in India. Lockheed Martin has also signed an agreement with Tata Advanced Systems Limited (TASL) to manufacture F-16 wings in India for global requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Airforce Chief in US
item.description: Air Chief Marshal AP Singh, Chief of the Air Staff #IAF, on an official visit to USA, visited Peterson Space Force Base. He interacted with General Gregory M Guillot (Commander US-NORTHCOM/NORAD) for a productive exchange of perspectives on complex operational modalities, reflecting the strength of the growing partnership.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Kongsberg Maritime gets Waterjet contract for Indian Navy’s Next Generation Missile Vessels
item.description: Kongsberg Maritime has signed a contract to supply 18 large Kamewa waterjets for the Indian Navy’s Next Generation Missile Vessel (NGMV) programme. Each of the vessels will feature waterjets, delivering exceptional speed and manoeuvrability for high-performance naval operations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Govt plans design, development of unmanned combat search and rescue aircraft
item.description: The government has envisioned design and development of an unmanned combat search and rescue aircraft for the IAF that can be used in missions to rescue aircrew without risking piloted aircraft, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cabinet approves Investment Proposal for construction of 1200 MW Kalai-II Hydro Electric Project in Anjaw District of Arunachal Pradesh with an outlay of Rs.14105.83 crore
item.description: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs, chaired by the Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi has approved investment of Rs.14,105.83 crore for construction of Kalai-II Hydro Electric Project (HEP) on Lohit river in Anjaw District of Arunachal Pradesh. The estimated completion period for the project is 78 months.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Army chief visits HAL, takes sortie in LCH Prachand
item.description: Army Chief Gen Upendra Dwivedi on Wednesday visited state-run defence major Hindustan Aeronautics Limited in Bengaluru, and also took a maiden sortie in Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) Prachand, officials said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: National Quantum Mission Clocks 1,000-Km Secure Network In Under 3 Years: MoS
item.description: India’s ambitious National Quantum Mission has achieved a major breakthrough, building a 1,000-kilometre secure quantum communication network in less than three years of its launch, Union Minister of State Dr. Jitendra Singh said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: admin
